children.text: This guide skips generic advice and focuses on the apps modern companies actually use.

stacks.name: AI-Powered

children.text: If you’re building your stack entirely with AI-native tools, start here.

stacks.name: Startup (Complete)
stacks.description: Operations, finance, and growth tools to run your startup.

children.text: After testing 100+ tools, building our own startup, and investing in 30+ others, these are the tool that most founders use and love.

stacks.name: Startup Operations
stacks.description: The right setup turns customer feedback into product decisions and actually keeps everyone aligned.

children.text: Every founder hits this point.
Your inbox becomes your help desk. Slack turns into your task manager.
Every day feels like catch-up, marking messages unread just to remember who's still waiting on you.
We've tested hundreds of tools, building our own startup and investing in 30+ others, and I've seen this pattern over and over.
The problem isn’t your team, it’s the chaos. The right setup turns customer feedback into product decisions and actually keeps everyone aligned.
Let’s break down the tools that make it possible.

stacks.name: Startup Finance
stacks.description: Most founders think finance mistakes are just part of the journey, they're not.

children.text: You need a way to take payments, pay people, and track where every dollar’s going.
We’ve spoken with hundreds of founders, made the same mistakes ourselves, and finally figured out what actually works. Here's the finance stack we landed on.
Every founder dreams of hiring a great growth marketer.
But here’s the truth: even when you find that person, you might be the one slowing them down.
I’ve been that technical founder, building everything from our website to email flows in custom code, until every marketing tweak needed my attention.
After investing in 30+ startups and talking with hundreds of founders, I’ve realized it’s the same story everywhere...you need to get out of your marketing team's way.
Here are the tools that make it possible.

Superhuman simply helps make checking your emails less dreadful and more enjoyable. Every action, including loading, is nearly instantaneous (unlike Gmail). It has a minimal and beautifully designed interface, along with quick shortcut keys to get through your inbox more quickly. We've used Superhuman for 6+ years and definitely think it's worth it compared to using Gmail.
If you're unsure, grab a
Superhuman Mail free trial
(30–days free) for yourself if it's worth the time savings for you.

Mercury Bank has a more modern banking experience with top-notch QuickBooks Online integration, great UI, and a strong feature set, while Novo frustrated me with clunky integrations and felt limited for anything beyond very small businesses.
Only use Novo if you're a freelancer or solopreneur with a small account and can't use Mercury Bank for some reason.
We love Copper & folk! We recommend folk for teams who are new to using a CRM (perhaps have never used one before and want something super simple and quick to setup).
We recommend Copper for teams with more established processes who are looking to automate their processes and integrate their CRM with other third party software.

app.name: Motion
app.description: Use AI to plan your work, automatically. Be 137% more productive.

app.name: Copper
app.description: The CRM for small + medium teams (200 or less) that use Google Workspace.
